creator: rometravelers
caption:
Took me 28 years to get to Italy. This boy has been on the planet for just over a year and is already being chauffeured around Rome in a vintage Fiat like a tiny Roman emperor.

I spent my first year eating pureed peas and crying.

He’s on a gelato tour of Europe.

We are not the same. 👶🏻

But honestly? Watching him take in Rome from the back of a vintage Fiat with the wind in his (very little) hair might be the best thing I’ve ever done as a mum. The cobblestones, the chaos, the Colosseum passing by. He had absolutely no idea what he was looking at and yet somehow looked completely unbothered and completely at home.

If you are heading to Rome with little ones, or honestly without, a vintage Fiat tour is the move. It’s one of those experiences that just feels different. Not museum-different. Not tourist-trap-different. Actually, genuinely, “I can’t believe I’m here” different. Rome hits harder from an open vintage car with a local who knows every corner of this city.

Thank you @experiencesrome for arranging this beautiful afternoon in Rome 🇮🇹
